Creamy Tuscan Chicken Pasta 🍝 Ingredients 1 chicken breast (cut in half) ½ tbsp paprika ½ tbsp garlic powder ½ tbsp onion powder 1 tsp salt 1 tsp black pepper Dash of olive oil 3 cups cooked pasta 2 tbsp olive oil 2 tbsp butter 1 small onion, diced 3 garlic cloves, minced 1½ cups cherry tomatoes, halved 1 tbsp tomato paste ⅓ cup sun-dried tomatoes 1 tbsp Italian seasoning 1 tbsp paprika Salt and pepper to taste ¾ cup heavy cream ½ cup pasta water 2 cups spinach (or less if preferred) 1 cup grated Parmesan cheese Instructions: - Season chicken with paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and black pepper. - Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the chicken and cook on both sides until golden brown and the internal temperature reaches 165°F. Remove from the pan and set aside. - In the same pan, add butter and diced onion. Cook until onions are translucent, then add minced garlic and cook for about 1 minute. - Stir in cherry tomatoes and cook for 2–3 minutes until softened. Add tomato paste and sun-dried tomatoes, stirring for another minute. - Add Italian seasoning, paprika, salt, and pepper. Cook for 1 minute, then pour in the heavy cream and pasta water. Stir well and let it simmer for 2 minutes. - Add spinach and Parmesan cheese. Stir until the cheese is melted and the sauce is creamy. - Slice the cooked chicken and return it to the pan. Let it simmer briefly to absorb the flavors, then add the cooked pasta. Toss until everything is evenly coated. - Garnish with extra Parmesan and serve warm and enjoy! #pasta #chicken  #ho#homemadefoodunch #dinner

Sheet Pan Chicken Taco Lettuce Boats I forget how ridiculously good this recipe is every single time I make it. Crispy taco chicken, chili-lime street corn, all the cold toppings, and somehow you still feel like a functioning adult afterward instead of needing a nap. And yes, I used queso fresco. Mind your business.                    Ingredients For the chicken: * 2½ lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs, chopped small * 2 packets taco seasoning * 1 teaspoon garlic powder * 1 teaspoon smoked paprika * Juice of 1 lime For the chili-lime corn: * 4 fresh ears of corn, kernels shaved off * ½ purple onion, diced * 1 tablespoon olive oil * 1 teaspoon chili powder * Salt to taste * Juice of ½–1 lime * ½ cup queso fresco * Fresh cilantro, chopped For serving: * Green leaf lettuce (sandwich cut) * Diced avocado * Pico de gallo * Jalapeño slices * Crema or Greek yogurt * Extra cilantro Instructions 1. Preheat oven to 425°F and line a sheet pan with parchment paper. 2. Toss chopped chicken with taco seasoning, garlic powder, smoked paprika, and lime juice. 3. Spread chicken onto one side of the sheet pan in an even layer. No oil needed. 4. Bake for 10 minutes. 5. Toss corn and purple onion with olive oil, chili powder, and salt. 6. Remove the pan, push chicken to one side, and add the corn mixture to the empty side. 7. Return to oven for another 10 minutes, then broil the last 3 minutes for crispy edges and charred corn. 8. Remove from oven and squeeze lime juice over the corn. Top with queso fresco and cilantro. 9. Assemble lettuce boats with chicken, street corn, avocado, pico, jalapeños, crema, and extra cilantro #taco #dinner #recipe #sheetpandinner #chicken

At its core, it’s a simple combination of slow-cooked beef and mashed potato, but like many French classics, the magic comes from the attention paid to each individual component. In this version I make a few tweaks to the traditional recipe whilst staying true to the components that make it so special. The result sits somewhere between a great cottage pie and a luxurious beef bourguignon. Deeply savoury, rich without feeling heavy and exactly the sort of dish that rewards a slow Sunday afternoon in the kitchen. For the full recipe, head over to Substack and hit subscribe - link in bio. NF x

d: Soy Beans Ep.2 Tofu Tofu Recipe Ingredients * Dried Soybeans: 500g (Soak in 2.5x the weight of dry beans) * Water: 2000g (4x the weight of dry beans) * Gansu (Nigari) : 20~25g (4~5% of the dry soy bean weight) Instructions 1. Soak dried soybeans and water in a 1:2.5 ratio for 10 hours 2. Blend 3. Strain -> Solid (biji) + Liquid (soy milk) 4. Boil the soy milk (to remove trypsin inhibitors, eliminate the raw beany taste, and ensure proper protein coagulation) 5. When it reaches 80~85°C, turn off the heat and add gansu 6. Stir and let it sit for 10 minutes 7. Prepare a mold and cheesecloth, then pour tofu into it 8. Press with a heavy weight for 10 minutes to finish the tofu Gansu (Nigari) * What is gansu : A liquid rich in minerals such as calcium and sulfates * Where does it comes from: It is collected during the sea salt production process, where minerals like calcium and sulfates are separated over 5 years

d: Mac n cheese with garlic butter steak bites 🤤 RECIPE⬇️ Mac n cheese: * 12 oz pasta * Chicken bouillon (for boiling pasta) * 10 oz sharp white cheddar * 8 oz Colby Jack * 5-6 oz Mozzarella  * 2 oz Parmesan * 3 tbs butter * 3 tbs flour * 1 cup heavy cream (1.5 if you prefer saucier) * 1 12-oz can evaporated milk * 1 tsp Dijon mustard * 1 tsp garlic powder * 1 tsp paprika * Salt and pepper to taste Garlic butter steak bites: * 1 lb steak, cut into bite size cubes  * 1 tsp each: garlic powder, smoked paprika, Worcestershire * 1/2 tsp Montreal steak seasoning * 1/2 tsp dried thyme * Drizzle of oil  * Salt and pepper to taste * 1/2 stick butter * 1/4 small shallot, minced * 4-5 garlic cloves, minced * Optional: chopped parsley for garnish 1. In a bowl, mix the steak with some oil and all the seasonings. Let that marinate while you prep the mac. 2. Shred cheeses and mix together. Set aside. 3. Boil pasta in water that has been generously seasoned with chicken bouillon or salt until al dente, 1-2 min below box instructions. 4. Melt butter and bloom the spices - garlic powder, paprika - for 1 min. Then add the flour and cook for a 1-2 mins. 5. While whisking, slowly add in the heavy cream and evaporated milk. Whisk until smooth.  6. Once the mixture warms up again (but NOT simmering) turn off the heat and stir in half of your cheese mixture in batches until combined. 7. Layer in a baking dish, starting with half the mac mixture, half of the leftover cheese, the rest of the mac, and finally the rest of the cheese. Sprinkle some paprika on top for color. 8. Bake at 375F for 25-30 min until golden. Broil on low for a few min for more crust if desired. 9. While the mac is baking, add oil to a pan and sear the steak until desired doneness and it develops a nice crust. 10. 1 min before the steak is done, add 1/2 stick butter, garlic, and shallots and cook for the remaining time. 11. When mac is done baking, rest 10 min before serving and top with the steak bites. #macandcheese #steakbites #garlicbutter #creamypasta

d: Creamy Tomato Pasta with Wagyu Steak 🍝 🥩  Ingredients:                                      •Olive oil and butter • 1 Ib pasta of choice • 2 shallots, finely chopped • 5 garlic cloves, finely chopped • 2 tbsp tomato paste • 1 cup heavy cream • ½-1 cup pasta water • 1 cup freshly grated Parmigiano Reggiano, plus more for serving • Fresh basil for garnish • 1 wagyu steak • 1 tbsp olive oil • Salt & pepper to taste • 1 tsp chili flakes • 1 tsp Italian seasoning Method: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pasta until al dente. Reserve about 1 cup of pasta water before draining. In a large pan over medium heat, add the olive oil, shallots, and garlic. Sauté until soft and fragrant. Stir in the tomato paste and let it cook for 2-3 minutes until rich and deep in color. Season with salt, pepper, chili flakes, and Italian seasoning. Pour in the heavy cream and mix until smooth. Add about ½ cup pasta water and stir. Add the grated Parmigiano Reggiano and mix until the sauce becomes creamy and silky. Add the cooked pasta directly into the sauce and toss until fully coated. Add more pasta water as needed to loosen the sauce. Season the wagyu steak with salt, pepper, and a touch of olive oil. Sear in a hot pan for a few minutes per side until beautifully caramelized and cooked to your liking. Let rest, then slice. Serve the pasta in bowls and top with sliced wagyu steak, fresh basil, and extra Parmigiano Reggiano. #wagyu #steak #pasta #food #asmr

d: pls lmk any tips + what i should make next time !!! def not perfect but it was my first go at chicken tinga tostadas so im proud 🙂🌮🤍 inspired by @Aelen  🤍 !!! here’s my recipe… approx macros for 2 tostadas: 460 kcal 34g protein 65g carbs 10g fat ingredients: 4 chicken breasts 4 roma tomatoes 1 medium onion 6 garlic cloves 3 bay leaves chicken bouillon powder 3 chipotle peppers in adobo olive oil corn tortillas fat free refried beans plain nonfat greek yogurt iceberg lettuce salt & pepper instructions: boil chicken breasts, roma tomatoes, half the onion, 3-4 garlic cloves, bay leaves, and a couple tbsp of chicken bouillon in a big pot for ~15 min. pull the chicken out and save the broth. blend the boiled tomatoes, onion, and garlic with 3 chipotle peppers, a scoop or two of broth, and another spoonful of bouillon until smooth. shred the chicken (kitchenaid paddle attachment makes this so easy). heat olive oil in a large pan, sauté the remaining diced onion and 2 minced garlic cloves until soft. add shredded chicken + the blended sauce. season with salt & pepper and let it simmer. for the tostadas — spray corn tortillas with olive oil, sprinkle with salt, and bake at 375° for ~15 min until crispy. assemble: refried beans → greek yogurt → chicken tinga → shredded iceberg. done. #highproteinmeals #mexicanfood #tostada #chickentinga #easyrecipe

d: These Bbq Bacon Cheeseburger Sliders made with @Godshall’s Beef Bacon are super easy to make. Perfect for a weeknight meal or really anytime!🔥 Ingredients: Godshalls Beef Bacon 1 lb ground beef 1 sweet onion (sliced) 1/2 bottle bbq sauce Burger Seasoning Hawaiian Sweet Rolls or slider buns 6 slices cheddar cheese Shredded Monterrey Jack 1 stick butter Course Salt Pepper Garlic Directions: Start off by cutting up the bacon into pieces. Throw it into a cold pan and cook down until just crispy. Set it aside.  Next cook down the onion in some bacon fat. Once the onion is almost done, throw in the ground beef. Chop it up, season it, and cook it down. Blot up some of the grease.  Then add the bacon back in with some bbq sauce. Give it a good mix.  Next slice the rolls in half and butter. Throw under the broiler for a few minutes to toast. ( don’t let them burn).  Once they’re done, preheat your oven to 350F degrees.  Add the sliced cheese to the bottom buns followed by  the burger mixture, and shredded cheese. Melt the other part of your butter. Add the top buns and brush with the butter. Season with the salt pepper garlic.  Put on a sheet pan and bake for 12 minutes. Enjoy.  #sliders #bacon #cheeseburger

d: Made with Marcangelo Mild Italian Sausage from @foodlion, this one comes together so easily but tastes like something you'd order at a restaurant. In the same pan, sauté butter, shallots, and garlic. Season with dried oregano, garlic salt, and Kinder's Butter seasoning. Add heavy cream and a splash of pasta water and let it simmer until slightly thickened. Toss in cooked rigatoni, add the sausage back in, and mix until coated. Finish with Parmesan cheese and red pepper flakes. Save this for your next cozy dinner night and grab your ingredients on your next Food Lion run. #pasta #pastarecipe #dinnerideas #easyrecipes #sponsored

d: Butter Chicken Recipe 🍛🔥👇🏼  For chicken:   2 chicken breast   3 tbsp Greek yogurt   Minced ginger   1 tsp garlic powder, paprika, cayenne, salt, cumin   1/2 lemon   For sauce:   1 tbsp butter   1/2 onion   2 fresh cloves garlic   1/2 tsp paprika, cumin, salt and chicken bouillon powder (optional)   2 1/4 cup tomato passata   1-2 tsp sugar   1 cup heavy cream   2 tbsp butter   cilantro   First, we’re going to cut the chicken into small cubes and season it with the spices, fresh ginger, lemon, and Greek yogurt. Then in a pan, add oil and fry the chicken.   Meanwhile, finely chop the onion and garlic.   Once the chicken is ready, remove it from the pan. In the same pan, add a small piece of butter and fry the onion, garlic, and spices together for 5 minutes over low heat until softened. Then add the tomato passata, sugar, and heavy cream. Mix very well and cook for a couple of minutes, then add two small pieces of butter to make it extra creamy. Finally, add the fried chicken back in, mix everything together, and top with cilantro and a little heavy cream. Serve it with white rice and naan bread.   👀 Remember that seasonings are always to taste, so don’t be afraid to add more if needed. If it tastes too acidic from the tomato sauce, don’t forget to add sugar to balance the flavors. #butterchicken #easyrecipe #foodie

d: Crispy Chicken Shawarma Wraps 🔥 For all of you asking about my recipe organizing app, search “Osta” on the app or google play store 🫶 Juicy chicken shawarma, creamy garlic sauce and crispy fries wrapped into one perfect bite 🌯 For the Chicken: -1.5 lbs (680g) skinless chicken thighs, sliced into strips -Seasoning: 1 tbsp paprika, 1 tbsp cumin, 1 tbsp onion powder, 2 tsp coriander, 1 tsp allspice, 2 tsp turmeric, 2 tsp black pepper, 1 tsp salt -3 tbsp olive oil -8 garlic cloves, minced -2 tbsp tomato paste -1/3 cup Greek yogurt -½ lemon, juiced For the Onions: -1 red onion, thinly sliced -2 tbsp olive oil -½ lemon, juiced -1/4 cup fresh parsley, finely chopped -1 tsp salt, 1 tsp black pepper, 2 tsp sumac For the Garlic Sauce: -1/2 cup Greek yogurt -1/2 cup mayonnaise -3 garlic cloves, minced -1/4 lemon, juiced For the Red Sauce Glaze: -1/4 cup olive oil + 2 tbsp tomato paste -1 tbsp pomegranate molasses (optional) -1 tsp paprika For Assembly: -3 Roma tomatoes, diced -Pitas -Frozen french fries Instructions: 1. In a bowl, combine the sliced chicken, spices, olive oil, garlic, tomato paste, yogurt, and lemon juice. Mix well & marinate for as long as your patience can handle, up to overnight. 2. In a large pan over medium-high heat, add a layer of olive oil. Once hot, add the chicken. Cook undisturbed for 3 mins, then cook for 6–7 more mins, stirring often, until golden & cooked through. 3. In a bowl, combine the sliced red onion, olive oil, lemon juice, parsley, salt, pepper, and sumac. Toss well, set aside. 4. In a bowl, mix the Greek yogurt, mayo, garlic, and lemon juice. Set aside in fridge. 5. In a bowl, whisk olive oil, tomato paste, pomegranate molasses (if using), & paprika. 6. Prepare your French fries however you like. Assemble the wraps: spread a generous layer of garlic sauce onto each pita. Top with  chicken, French fries, diced tomatoes & the onions. Wrap tightly. 7. Melt butter in a pan over low heat. Place the wraps seam-side down & brush the tops with the red sauce. Cook until crispy golden, flip, brush the other side, & repeat. Top with more garlic sauce. Enjoy!! #recipesoftiktok #dinnerideas #shawarma #viral #chicken

d: 🎀 LAZY GIRL DINNER 🎀 One pot creamy paprika beef pasta 🍝  Ingredients: 350g rigatoni (uncooked)  200ml double cream 600ml beef stock  1 cup tomato passata  2 tbs tomato paste  1 tbs smoked paprika 1 tsp cayenne pepper 1 1/2 tbs Italian herbs 2 tsp chilli flakes (optional) 1 brown onion, finely diced 4 garlic cloves, minced  500g beef mince (I used 15% fat) Salt & pepper, to taste  Method:  * On a medium to high heat add your olive oil to your pan before frying off your beef mince until brown. Add in your spices, onion and garlic and stir until softened and fragrant (about 5 minutes). * Add your tomato paste and stir through until combined - cook for about 2 minutes. * Add your passata and cook through for an additional 5 minutes before adding your stock, cream and finally your rigatoni. Stir through until the rigatoni is lightly covered by the liquid. * Turn heat down to medium/low and place lid on for 15 minutes or until til cooked through.  * Remove lid and stir through your Parmesan and serve 😊

d: At this point we ALL need to work together to lower costs for Georgians ! #ossof #healthyrecipes #cajunchicken #onepot #foodie Cajun Chicken & Rice  Ingredients: Chicken marinated:  2 Lbs Bonless skinless chicken thighs  1/2 Tbsp Sazon tropical  1/2 Tbsp Smoked paprika  1/2 Tbsp ( low sodium) cajun seasoning  2 Tbsp Olive oil  Other ingredients  1 tbsp Olive oil  1 tbsp butter  1/2 red or yellow onion diced  2 Tbsp Minced garlic  2 cup cooked white rice  Cream Sauce  2 Tbsp Butter  1/2 a lemons juice  1 Tbsp Garlic powder  2 Tsp Tablespoons cajan seasoning  1/2 tbsp Smoked paprika  1 cup Heavy cream  Instructions: 1. Start by marinating your thighs with the chicken marinade above! (allow to marinate for thirty minutes to twenty four hours in the refrigerator) 2. After the chicken is marinated melt down butter in a pan along with olive oil,  3. Once  your butter begins to bubble add in your chicken and cook on each side for 5 to 7 minutes on medium heat for a total of 10 to 14 minutes or until internal temperature of 165° F 4.  Next remove your chicken from the pan 5. In clean pan melt down your butter add in your diced onions, minced garlic, and a pinch of Cajun seasoning & sauté for 60 seconds on medium heat  6. Once your garlic and onions become fragrant, add in cooked rice and mix until all your rice is well coated with seasoning and remove from heat Cream sauce  1. For your cream sauce in a medium sauce pan, meltdown,your butter over medium to low heat, and adding all your ingredients for your cream sauce whisk and stir continuously until your sauce thickens, and is able to coat the back of your spoon 2. Add to chicken back into your rice and top with your cream sauce and enjoy!!

d: 🦞🧈Mini Lobster Rolls with Old Bay Aioli  This one is an unforgettable seafood dish that I love and soooo worth it; such a fun summer appetizer that will impress your guests... or yourself, bc it's THAT easy. 🔥Old Bay Aioli: Mix about 1/3 cup of mayo with a squeeze of lemon juice, one clove of minced garlic, and old bay, salt, pepper, paprika and garlic & onion powder. I didn't measure these, but maybe start with a 1/2 tablespoon of @OLD BAY Seasoning, adjust to your liking. I like a lot! 🦞 Lobster: Quick note on this: I made 4 rolls and used the rest of the lobster in a lobster salad. I probably could have gotten 8 mini rolls out of 2 tails, so keep that in mind! 👉🏼I cut 2 lobster tails into small chunks. Simmer a stick of butter (should be good butter, like @Kerrygold USA) over medium low heat, add in two slivered garlic cloves and simmer for a minute until fragrant. Add in the lobster, and season. I used salt, garlic & onion powder; this doesn't need a lot, I used about a tsp each.  🧈Gently poach the lobster in the butter until it is no longer translucent and is completely opaque. It should take about 3 minutes but may vary depending on your stove; as long as all of the lobster is opaque and the meat is firm but yields lightly when pressed, you're set.  🍞Brioche:  - Slice the sides off your mini brioche rolls.  - Wipe out the pan you poached the lobster in  - add a tbsp or 2 more butter - toast the rolls over medium heat on either side until golden. This can move quick so keep an eye on them.  Finally, stuff the rolls full of lobster, garnish with extra butter if you like (I'm not judging) and chives. These are delightful with caviar, not to mention impressive if you're serving to guests, so do whatever you like with that information 😌 #gourmetmeals #lobster #easylunchanddinnerideas #deliciousmeals #dinnercooking

d: It looks a bit of a mess, but it tasted absolutely incredible. Double Sided Bacon and Jalapeño Grilled Cheese 🧀  INGREDIENTS 2 slices of thick white Bread (I used Tiger Bread)  4 rashers of Smoked Streaky Bacon Pickled Jalapeños  Lots of Grated Mozzarella  Lots of Grated Cheddar Butter for Frying A little oil for the pan METHOD Throw your butter in a lightly oiled pan over a medium low heat until bubbling then go in with your bread to toast. Repeat on the other side then set aside Fry your bacon to your liking then line them up in the pan and add your jalapeños along with your cheeses  Lay your toasted bread over the top of the cheese, then add some more cheese to the top of one slice of bread and leave the cheese get crispy for a minute or 2 Flip one side of the bacon, jalapeños, cheese and bread over the other to make a sandwich and leave on a low heat, flipping regularly until the cheese in the middle melts Remove from the pan and enjoy!  #grilledcheese #easyrecipe #latenightsnack #baconandjalapeño #cheesetoastie

d: You HAVE to try the Japanese curry from One Piece 🏴‍☠️ What’s everyone watching right now? Let me know in the comments  Ingredients - chuck roast -1/3 cup lard - 1/3 cup flour - 2 tbsp Japanese curry powder - 1 tbsp garam masala - 1 Fuji apple - 40oz beef stock (not sponsored but I like to mix a tbsp of the @knorr beef base with water) - 4 small tomatoes - 2 yellow onions - 2 gold potatoes - 2 carrots - 1/2 cup Greek yogurt or kiefer  - 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste - 2 tsp turmeric - salt and pepper - medium grain rice First slice chuck roast into bite size pieces. Season with salt and pepper then add a heaping tbsp of ginger garlic paste and 2 tsp turmeric along with the Greek yogurt. Let marinade for however long your patience can tolerate.  make the roux by combining equal parts flour and lard, butter, or oil. Stir constantly until it darkens a bit. Not gumbo dark but somewhere between blond and chocolate.  Add in the curry powder (most places sell the @sbfoodsusa curry powder which is essential for Japanese curry. Again, not sponsored) and the garem masala. Stir to combine and set aside.  Simmer the tomatoes for a few minutes to make the skin easy to remove. Let cool and optionally scoop out the seeds. It doesn’t matter either way.  In a large heavy pot, sautee the diced onion until soft add in 1/4 cup sake. Cook until almost completely evaporated or omit this step if you can’t buy alcohol. Pour in the roux. Add in the tomatoes and beef along with beef stock. Let simmer gently for about 2 hours until the beef is tender but not falling apart. Lastly add in the chopped potatoes and carrots and grate 1 Fuji apple directly in. Let simmer until the vegetables are tender and serve with rice! #onepiece #japanesecurry #sanji #animefood #fyp

d: After years of living in China, I’ve honestly come to see street food as better than restaurant food most of the time. Of course there are exceptions, but the best street vendors usually have a personal stake in every dish they serve. They’re cooking the same handful of specialties day after day, refining them over years, sometimes decades. There’s a sense of pride and care that’s hard to replicate in a larger restaurant. And beyond the food itself, there’s something deeply satisfying about eating on the street. No fancy décor, no presentation tricks—just great food, fresh air, and everyday life happening around you. It feels more honest, more connected to the place and the people who live there. Restaurants can offer comfort and occasion, but street food often offers something rarer: authenticity. #china #chinesefood #streetfood #familytime #chinalife
zh: 在中国生活多年后，我真心觉得街头小吃大多数时候比餐厅更好。摊主亲手打磨同一道菜，历经数年甚至数十年，那份用心难以复制。街头吃饭没有繁琐装饰，只有美食、新鲜空气和烟火气，更真实、更接地气。餐厅给人舒适与仪式感，街头小吃却往往给人更难得的东西：真实。

d: @realpicklelife is doing a dance to a slowed version of Wiggle by Jason Derulo featuring Snoop Dogg, turning a real-life pickle into a full throwback dance meme. The slowed-down beat, funny pickle movement, and classic Wiggle hook make this feel smooth, ridiculous, and instantly ready for TikTok green screen edits. What makes this work is the contrast. Wiggle already has that playful 2010s party energy, but slowing it down and giving the spotlight to @realpicklelife makes the pickle look weirdly dramatic, confident, and locked into every move. It has that perfect “why does this pickle actually go hard?” vibe where nostalgia, dance humor, and pickle aura all collide. Use this CapCut template for green screen jokes, pickle memes, dance remakes, or any post where you want something random to carry the whole beat. It works especially well for slowed Wiggle edits, Jason Derulo memes, Snoop Dogg sound posts, funny reaction videos, and TikTok FYP clips where the pickle steals the entire vibe. d: Iron Man: Analog Horror. Part 4. The Boy Who Saved New York. Attention!!! Emergency Message!!!!! Tony Stark — the hero of America. The only superhero in history since Captain America in 1945. He became a national symbol, saving millions of lives in New York City from the terror of the Green Monster known as the Hulk. When the experiments of the brilliant scientist Bruce Banner spiraled out of control and he turned into something destructive, 16-year-old Tony Stark built an iron suit in his uncle’s garage. It was powered by a homemade nuclear reactor. This invention was what brought an end to the terror. After that, Stark became the most famous man in the country. He graduated with honors and founded a company focused on robotics and artificial intelligence — Stark Industries. By the age of 20, he had become the richest man in America. His technologies were used in the military, medicine, education, and IT. A billionaire. A philanthropist. A playboy. And a man who never wore the Iron Man suit again… until recently. On his 32nd birthday, Stark hosted a racing event in Monaco. During the race, he lost control and crashed off the track. Diagnosis: multiple fractures and severe traumatic brain injury. Doctors stated that his chances of survival were minimal. Even if he survived, he would likely remain in a vegetative state. But two months later, he appeared in public. Completely healthy. No explanation was given. According to unofficial sources, an experimental Stark Industries technology was used in his treatment — organoids. Living neural structures. How exactly they were applied remains unknown. After his recovery, Stark’s behavior began to change. Employees reported sudden shifts — from calm to aggressive within seconds. His speech became harsh. His actions, impulsive. At times, it felt as if they were dealing not with an adult man, but with a teenager. Then the incident happened. May 12. 7:00 PM. Manhattan. Tony Stark appeared in the city… wearing the old Iron Man suit. That night, 7 people were killed. The bodies were found in various conditions: crushed, torn apart, burned. Some were partially vaporized. The use of a built-in flamethrower was confirmed. The military attempted to intervene. Unsuccessful. Conventional weapons caused no significant damage to the suit. The use of heavy artillery was deemed impossible due to the risk of reactor detonation. An explosion could destroy a significant portion of the city. Later, it was discovered: the reactor emits radiation. According to experts, prolonged exposure inside the suit would lead to acute radiation sickness, organ failure, and eventual death of the wearer. However, there is a problem. According to intelligence reports, Stark does not remove the suit. Ever. This made it impossible to neutralize him during sleep. A previous special forces operation resulted in the deaths of three soldiers. At this point, the only strategy is to wait. To wait for the radiation to do what weapons could not. The city waits. And tries to understand what caused the changes in Stark’s behavior… and why he no longer takes off the suit. #IronMan #AnalogHorror #TonyStark #Marvel #Horror
zh: 钢铁侠：模拟恐怖·第4部·拯救纽约的少年·紧急警报！！！托尼·斯塔克——美国英雄。自1945年美国队长以来历史上唯一的超级英雄。他成为国家象征，将数百万纽约市民从被称为绿巨人浩克的恐怖中拯救出来。当天才科学家布鲁斯·班纳的实验失控、他变成了一头破坏性怪物时，16岁的托尼·斯塔克在叔叔的车库里造出了一套钢铁战甲，以自制核反应堆为动力，终结了这场恐怖。此后，斯塔克成为全国最知名的人物，以优异成绩毕业，创立了专注于机器人和人工智能的斯塔克工业公司。20岁时，他已成为美国首富，其技术被广泛应用于军事、医疗、教育和信息技术领域。亿万富翁、慈善家、花花公子，以及一个再未穿上钢铁战甲的男人……直到最近。32岁生日当天，斯塔克在摩纳哥举办赛车活动，赛中失控冲出赛道。诊断结果：多处骨折及严重颅脑损伤。医生表示其生存概率极低，即便存活，极可能长期植物人状态。然而两个月后，他出现在公众视野中，完全康复，毫无解释。据非官方消息，其治疗采用了斯塔克工业的实验性技术——类器官，即活体神经结构，具体应用方式不明。康复后，斯塔克的行为开始改变：员工反映他会在数秒内从平静转为暴躁，言辞粗暴，行为冲动，有时令人感觉面对的不是一个成年男性，而是一个少年。随后事件发生。5月12日，晚7时，曼哈顿，托尼·斯塔克身穿旧款钢铁战甲现身于城市。当晚7人遇难，遗体以各种状态被发现：被压碎、撕裂、烧焦，部分甚至被汽化，内置火焰喷射器的使用得到证实。军方介入，未能成功，常规武器对战甲几乎无法造成有效损伤，重型火炮因反应堆爆炸风险而无法使用，一旦爆炸可能摧毁城市大片区域。随后发现：反应堆持续释放辐射，据专家称，长期置身战甲内部将导致急性放射病、器官衰竭并最终死亡。然而问题在于，据情报显示，斯塔克从不脱下战甲，这使得趁其睡眠期间将其制服成为不可能，此前一次特种部队行动造成三名士兵阵亡。目前唯一的策略是等待，等待辐射完成

d: Cheesecake the capybara has really taken to sneaking over first thing in the morning while Pumpkin’s still in bed so that she gets the couch to herself 😂 Pumpkin and Cheesecake, our two capybaras, have access to our back porch through a connected pasture and will sometimes come up to the door for some extra attention and cuddles. Though they look cute, and I do let them in when I can, they are extremely time consuming and expensive to care for. When you see clips of them coming in the house, when I say “don’t eat anything” I mean it. They’re the largest rodent in the world, and will chew on pretty much anything, so I have to watch them 100% of the time when they’re inside. They’re also smart, and she will wait until I’m not paying attention to try to chew on wires or the blankets on the couch 🤦🏼‍♀️ #capybara #funnyanimals #funny #animals
zh: 芝士蛋糕（卡皮巴拉）每天早上趁南瓜还在床上时悄悄溜过来，好独占沙发。南瓜和芝士蛋糕是我养的两只卡皮巴拉，它们可以通过牧场进入后廊，有时会来门口撒娇求抱抱。虽然它们很可爱，但饲养非常耗时耗钱。每当它们进屋，我说"别乱咬东西"是认真的——它们是世界上最大的啮齿动物，几乎什么都啃，所以在室内必须全程盯着，稍不注意就会去啃电线或沙发毯子。

d: Cowboy butter steak bites ✨ My husband ate both of these plates and rated it a 10/10 😭 Ingredients  Steak - 2 pounds ribeye or sirloin steak, cubed - 1 tbsp neutral oil - 2 tsp Worcestershire sauce  - 2 tsp garlic powder  - Salt and pepper to taste (generous amount) “Caramelized” onions  - 1 large yellow onion, sliced - 1 tsp granulated sugar  - Heaping pinch of salt Cowboy butter - 12 tbsp unsalted butter - 5 garlic cloves, minced  - 1 shallot, minced  - 1 lemon, freshly squeezed - 1 tbsp Dijon mustard  - 1 heaping tbsp parsley, chopped - 1 heaping tbsp chives, chopped - 1 tsp thyme - 1 tsp cayenne powder (adjust to heat preference  - 1/2 tsp red chili flakes - 1/2 tsp smoked paprika  - Salt to taste  Instructions  - Toss cubed steak with oil, Worcestershire, garlic powder, and a generous amount of salt & pepper. Set aside while the pan heats. - Heat a skillet over medium heat with a little oil or butter. - Add onions, sugar, and salt. Cook 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until soft, golden, and jammy. Remove from pan and set aside. - Crank heat to medium-high/high. Add steak in a single layer (work in batches if needed). - Sear 2–3 minutes per side until deeply browned. Remove and set aside. Lower heat to medium. - Add butter to the same pan. Once melted, add garlic and shallot — cook ~30 seconds until fragrant. - Stir in lemon juice, Dijon, parsley, chives, thyme, cayenne, chili flakes, smoked paprika, and salt. - Add steak back to the pan. - Toss to coat in the butter sauce for 1–2 minutes. - Serve over creamy mashed potatoes and enjoy! • • • #recipe #steak #dinnerideas #food #steak

d: Taco Loaded Potato Bowl 🌮🥔🔥 Doesn't get much better than this... easy, healthy and PACKED with flavor! Perfect quick simple delicious post workout meal, lunch or dinner! Great for meal prepping as well!  FULL RECIPE ⬇️ INGREDIENTS ~1.5 lbs Yukon gold potatoes  - 1 lb lean ground beef (Used 93/7)  - Extra virgin olive oil  - Taco Seasoning of choice  (I used @Grillin With Dad's)  Pico De Gallo (Combine all ingredients)  - 1 cup fresh diced tomatoes - 1/2 cup diced red onion  - 1 small jalapeño diced - (Remove seeds unless you want it spicy) - 1/2 cup chopped cilantro  - Juice of 1/2 a lime  - Salt to taste   Guacamole (Combine all ingredients)  - 2 medium sized avocados  - 1/2 cup diced red onion  - Juice of a lime  - 1/2 cup cilantro chopped  - Garlic powder to taste  - Salt to taste  Note: The measurements are estimated. Adjust ingredients to your liking.  Addition toppings: - Shredded Cheese - Sourcream - Cilantro  DIRECTIONS  1.  Prepare the Pico and guacamole then set in fridge.  2. Wash & dry the potatoes then dice them up into small cubes. Put them in a large bowl then add in olive oil and taco seasoning. Mix till well combined then place evenely on a lined baking sheet. Bake at 425 for ~30-35 minutes. Give a quick mix half way. 3. While the potatoes are in the oven, prep your ground beef. Add the ground beef to a greased large skillet over medium-high heat. Break it up then add in taco seasoning. Mix and add more if needed. I also like to add a bit of water or broth for extra moisture. Cook for 8-10 minutes mixing frequently until done to your liking.  4. Remove potatoes from oven and let cool for a few minutes.   5. Assemble your bowl & ENJOY! 🙌🏻 • • • • #explore #cooking #bhfyp #food #easyrecipe #simple #foodporn #healthy #tasty #healthyrecipes #calwillcookit #diet #healthylifestyle #mealprep #gains #weightloss #tacos #fitfood #highprotein

d: Beef Bourguignon🥩🍷 Ingredients (Serves 4–6) • 900g beef chuck, diced into large chunks • 450g double-smoked bacon, cut into lardons • 2 onions, sliced • 2–3 carrots, sliced • 2 tbsp tomato paste • 500ml red wine • 750ml beef stock • 2 sprigs rosemary • 3 sprigs thyme • 2 bay leaves • 225g mushrooms, roughly chopped • 150g pearl onions, peeled • Salt & black pepper • Olive oil To serve: • Mashed potatoes, crusty bread or buttered pasta • Fresh parsley (optional) Instructions 1. Brown the beef:     Season the beef generously with salt and pepper.     Heat a large Dutch oven or heavy pot over medium-high heat.     Sear the beef in batches until deeply browned on all sides.     Remove and set aside. 2. Cook the bacon:     Add the bacon and cook until crisp and golden.     Remove, leaving the fat in the pot. 3. Build the base:     Add the onions and carrots to the bacon fat.     Cook until softened.     Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1–2 minutes. 4. Deglaze:     Pour in the red wine and scrape up all the browned bits from the bottom of the pot.     Simmer for a few minutes. 5. Braise:     Return the beef and bacon to the pot.     Add the beef stock, rosemary, thyme and bay leaves.     Bring to a gentle simmer.     Cover and cook for 2½–3 hours until the beef is tender. 6. Roast the vegetables:     Toss the mushrooms and pearl onions with a little olive oil, salt and pepper.     Roast until golden and caramelized. 7. Finish:     Stir the mushrooms and pearl onions into the stew during the final hour of cooking.     Continue cooking until the beef falls apart easily and the sauce is rich and glossy. 8. Serve:     Spoon over mashed potatoes, buttered pasta or alongside crusty bread. #beefbourguignon #frenchfood #recipevideo #beefrecipe #ratatouillemovie
zh: 红酒炖牛肉，食材（4-6人份）：牛肩肉900g、双熏培根450g、洋葱2个、胡萝卜2-3根、番茄酱2汤匙、红葡萄酒500ml、牛肉高汤750ml、迷迭香2枝、百里香3枝、月桂叶2片、蘑菇225g、珍珠洋葱150g、盐和黑胡椒、橄榄油；配餐：土豆泥、硬皮面包或黄油意面、新鲜欧芹（可选）；做法：1.煎牛肉：用盐和黑胡椒充分腌制牛肉，用铸铁锅大火分批将牛肉四面煎至深褐色，取出备用。2.煸培根：放入培根煸至酥脆金黄，取出留油备用。3.炒底料：用培根油炒软洋葱和胡萝卜，加入番茄酱翻炒1-2分钟。4.收底汁：倒入红葡萄酒，刮起锅底焦香碎，炖煮几分钟。5.炖煮：将牛肉和培根放回锅中，加入高汤、迷迭香、百里香和月桂叶，小火慢炖2.5-3小时至牛肉软烂。6.烤蔬菜：蘑菇和珍珠洋葱拌橄榄油、盐和黑胡椒，烤至金黄焦糖化。7.收尾：在最后一小时将蘑菇和珍珠洋葱拌入炖锅，继续炖至牛肉入口即化、酱汁浓郁光亮。8.盛盘：搭配土豆泥、黄油意面或硬皮面包即可

d: Seared Snapper (or Dentex Bream?) with Crispy Fennel and Smoked Anchovy Cream Pea Purée I asked my fishmonger for snapper. He pointed to a dentex bream and said, “the Aussies would call that snapper.” And for a second, I couldn’t tell if he was educating me or just making the sale easier. Because the truth is - I didn’t actually know if he was right. And I’m not sure it mattered. The fish came home with me either way. There’s something slightly uncomfortable about how quickly a name settles in. It frames the way you cook it, the way you plate it, the way you taste it. Snapper felt intentional. Dentex feels interchangeable. And that small shift does more work than it should. Maybe he was right. And even if he wasn’t, it made me think about how often we trust the label more than the thing itself. How often we inherit a story and let it do the tasting for us. Either way, it ate well. Which makes it harder to tell whether the difference was ever in the fish at all. #storytelling #homecooking #reflection #fish #youarewhatyoueat

I Got Another Call From The Principal Apparently my school lunches are making the other parents look bad. Which feels a little dramatic if you ask me. So naturally I responded the only reasonable way possible… by making Nashville Hot Chicken Sliders. Crispy fried chicken tossed in homemade Nashville hot sauce piled onto soft slider buns and packed into a lunchbox that probably didn’t need this much effort. The principal says he has to take the complaints seriously. I respect that. He also said the sliders looked incredible. #lunch #chicken #food #cooking #homemade

Don’t leave Rome without trying these 5 street foods 🇮🇹🍝 Save for your trip 📌  Rome has some of the best street food in Italy – from crispy supplì and pizza al taglio to proper Roman sandwiches and fresh maritozzi. 📌 Add these to your list 👇 1️⃣ Supplì (Trastevere) 🧀 Fried rice balls with tomato & mozzarella  📍 Via di S. Francesco a Ripa, 137  2️⃣ Trapizzino (Testaccio) 🔥 Roman stuffed pizza pockets 📍 Via Giovanni Branca, 88 3️⃣ Casa Manco   🍕 Pizza al taglio – pizza by the slice & priced by weight.  📍In @mercatoditestaccio – Via Aldo Manuzio, 66C 4️⃣ Mordi e Vai   🥪 Panino Romanesco – one of the best sandwich spots many people miss in Rome 🔥  📍 Also in Testaccio Market – Nuovo Mercato Comunale di Testaccio BOX 15 5️⃣ Regoli  🥯 Maritozzi – brioche filled with fresh whipped cream 📍 Via dello Statuto, 60 (10min walk from Termini station) If you’re planning a Rome food trip, these are all worth adding to your list – especially if you want a mix of local Roman food, classic street food, and traditional spots locals actually eat at. 💡 Travel Tip – ad Use the @tripspire.app before your trip to help organise food spots you save from social media posts. You basically paste the video link into the app and it pulls the featured places into a list, making it much easier to keep track of everything you’ve saved for your trip. 📌 Save this Rome food guide for later 🇮🇹 👉 Follow us for more food guides around Europe. #romefood #romefoodguide #romeitaly #romanfood #streetfood
zh: 离开罗马前必吃的5种街头美食，收藏备用。罗马有意大利最棒的街头小吃，从脆炸饭团、切片披萨到正宗罗马三明治和新鲜奶油面包。逐一加入清单：1. Supplì（特拉斯提弗列区）——番茄芝士炸饭团，地址：Via di S. Francesco a Ripa 137；2. Trapizzino（台斯塔丘区）——罗马夹馅披萨口袋，地址：Via Giovanni Branca 88；3. Casa Manco——切片披萨，按重量计价，地址：台斯塔丘市场内 Via Aldo Manuzio 66C；4. Mordi e Vai——罗马风味三明治，许多人在罗马错过的最佳三明治之一，地址：台斯塔丘新市场15号摊位；5. Regoli——奶油布里欧面包，地址：Via dello Statuto 60（距特米尼火车站步行10分钟）。如果你在计划罗马美食之旅，这些地方都值得加入清单，尤其适合想体验本地罗马风味、经典街头小吃和当地人常去老店的旅行者。旅行建议：出发前使用Tripspire App整理你从社交媒体收藏的美食地点，粘贴视频链接即可自动提取地点列表，方便管理所有收藏。收藏这份罗马美食攻略，关注我们获取更多欧洲美食指南。
